evals.prompt: Format this draft as a blog post for the SensiSkin website. [Draft: 600-word text about dry skin care in winter, written in brand voice, covering causes, routine steps, and product types to use.]
evals.expected_output: Should structure the draft into the mandatory blog post format: H1 title (under 60 chars, no question, contains keyword), 80-120 word intro (opens with reader situation, no 'In this post...'), 3-4 H2 sections (statements not questions, 150-300 words each), closing section with specific CTA. Should add image placeholder after H1. Should add minimum 1-2 internal link placeholders. Should append META description draft under 155 characters at end. Should NOT rewrite the substance — same ideas, same voice, just structured. Should verify total word count is 800-1500.
assertions: Produces H1 title under 60 characters, not a question
Intro is 80-120 words and opens with reader situation
H2 headings are statements, not questions
Each body section is 150-300 words
Closing section has specific CTA, not 'I hope this helped'
Image placeholder added after H1
At least 1 internal link placeholder present
META description appended, under 155 characters
Total word count is 800-1500
Substance unchanged — same ideas as draft

evals.prompt: Adapt this blog post draft as a newsletter. [Draft: 900-word blog post about hyaluronic acid for sensitive skin.]
evals.expected_output: Should distill the 900-word blog to 350-500 words total. Should extract one core idea from the blog, not compress all three sections. Should produce 3 subject line options: Option A curiosity angle, Option B direct benefit, Option C personal/question angle — each under 45 characters, no emojis, no ALL CAPS. Should produce 1 preview text (60-90 chars, different from subject lines). Should format body as flowing paragraphs (no H2 headings), paragraphs max 3 sentences. Should include exactly one CTA. Should add personal sign-off. Should NOT include tables or complex formatting.
assertions: Total word count is 350-500
Produces exactly 3 subject line options with different angles
Each subject line is under 45 characters
Preview text is 60-90 characters and not a repeat of subject lines
Body has no H2 headings
No paragraph exceeds 3 sentences
Exactly one CTA
Includes personal sign-off
Focuses on one core idea, not a compressed summary of all blog sections
No tables or complex formatting

evals.prompt: Turn this into a Google Business Profile post. [Draft: 400-word article about the benefits of facial massage for lymphatic drainage, written for SensiSkin clients in Novi Sad.]
evals.expected_output: Should compress the draft to 900-1200 characters (hard limit 1500). Should open with a hook of 100-150 characters that works as a standalone teaser before 'Prikaži više'. Should develop one specific point in the body (600-800 characters). Should end with a specific CTA using a phone number as plain text. Should include a photo suggestion. Should state the character count in the delivery. Should NOT include hashtags. Should NOT include URLs or hyperlinks. Should flag if character count is between 1200-1500.
assertions: Total character count is under 1500
Target is 900-1200 characters
Opening line is 100-150 characters and works as standalone teaser
No hashtags present
No URLs or hyperlinks in text
CTA includes phone number as plain text
Photo suggestion included
Character count stated in delivery
Focuses on one topic only

evals.prompt: We have a draft about spring skin detox routines. Format it as all three: blog post, newsletter, and GBP post.
evals.expected_output: Should identify blog as the primary format and format it first. Should then adapt blog → newsletter by extracting one core idea and making it personal (not compressing everything). Should then adapt blog → GBP by finding the single sharpest point and compressing to character limit. Should NOT write three independent pieces from the draft. Should deliver all three clearly labeled. Each format should meet its own constraints (blog 800-1500 words, newsletter 350-500 words, GBP 900-1200 chars). Brand voice should be consistent across all three.
assertions: Produces all three formats
Blog is formatted first and fully
Newsletter distills to one core idea, not compresses all
GBP extracts the sharpest single point
All three derive from the same draft, not written independently
Blog meets 800-1500 word count
Newsletter meets 350-500 word count
GBP meets 900-1200 character target
Brand voice consistent across all three formats

evals.prompt: Format this as a newsletter. [Draft: 1200-word blog post about five different skincare ingredients, one per section, each with its own benefits and usage tips.]
evals.expected_output: Should recognize that adapting all five ingredient sections into a 350-500 word newsletter is impossible without distillation — this is not compression, it requires picking ONE ingredient or ONE angle. Should select the most engaging or seasonally relevant single angle. Should explicitly tell the user which angle was chosen and why. Should produce a newsletter at 350-500 words covering only that angle well. Should NOT attempt to mention all five ingredients shallowly. Should suggest the other four ingredients as future newsletter topics.
assertions: Recognizes that all five topics cannot fit in 350-500 words
Selects one topic or angle for the newsletter
Tells the user which angle was chosen and why
Newsletter word count is 350-500
Does not try to shallowly cover all five ingredients
Suggests other four as future newsletter topics
Produces 3 subject lines, preview text, single CTA

evals.prompt: Make this a GBP post. [Draft text that, when formatted, runs to approximately 1700 characters.]
evals.expected_output: Should count the characters and detect the post is over the 1500 hard limit (and well above the 900-1200 target). Should cut from the body only — never from the opening hook or CTA. Should NOT cut the CTA or shorten the opening below 100 characters. Should deliver the final post at 900-1200 characters. Should state both the original character count and the final character count. Should explain what was cut and why those specific parts were removed.
assertions: Detects character count is over the 1500 hard limit
Cuts from body section only
Opening hook remains 100-150 characters
CTA remains intact
Final post is 900-1200 characters
States original character count in delivery
States final character count in delivery
Explains what was cut
